   195→    }
   196→  }
   197→`)
   198→
   199→// ── Search page ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   200→
   201→export function Search() {
   202→  const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams()
   203→  const [selectedUnitID, setSelectedUnitID] = useState<string | null>(null)
   204→  const [detailOpened, setDetailOpened] = useState(false)
   205→  const [filtersOpened, { toggle: toggleFilters }] = useDisclosure(false)
   206→  const isDesktop = useMediaQuery('(min-width: 62em)')
   207→
   208→  // ── View mode state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   209→  const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState<ViewMode>(() => {
   210→    const fromUrl = searchParams.get('view')
   211→    if (fromUrl && VALID_VIEW_MODES.includes(fromUrl as ViewMode)) return fromUrl as ViewMode
   212→    const fromStorage = localStorage.getItem(VIEW_MODE_KEY)
   213→    if (fromStorage && VALID_VIEW_MODES.includes(fromStorage as ViewMode))
   214→      return fromStorage as ViewMode
   215→    return 'card'
   216→  })
   217→
   218→  const handleViewModeChange = useCallback(
   219→    (mode: ViewMode) => {
   220→      setViewMode(mode)
   221→      localStorage.setItem(VIEW_MODE_KEY, mode)
   222→      setSearchParams(
   223→        (prev) => {
   224→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   225→          if (mode === 'card') {
   226→            next.delete('view')
   227→          } else {
   228→            next.set('view', mode)
   229→          }
   230→          return next
   231→        },
   232→        { replace: true },
   233→      )
   234→    },
   235→    [setSearchParams],
   236→  )
   237→
   238→  const dataSources = useDataSources()
   239→
   240→  const [fieldsResult] = useQuery<{ searchFields: SearchField[] }>({
   241→    query: SearchFieldsQuery,
   242→  })
   243→
   244→  const [outputFieldsResult] = useQuery<{ searchOutputFields: SearchOutputField[] }>({
   245→    query: SearchOutputFieldsQuery,
   246→  })
   247→
   248→  const fields = useMemo(
   249→    () => fieldsResult.data?.searchFields ?? [],
   250→    [fieldsResult.data?.searchFields],
   251→  )
   252→
   253→  const allOutputFields = useMemo(
   254→    () => outputFieldsResult.data?.searchOutputFields ?? [],
   255→    [outputFieldsResult.data?.searchOutputFields],
   256→  )
   257→
   258→  // ── Column visibility state ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   259→  const [visibleColumns, setVisibleColumns] = useState<Set<string>>(() => {
   260→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(VISIBLE_COLUMNS_KEY)
   261→    if (stored) {
   262→      try {
   263→        const arr = JSON.parse(stored) as string[]
   264→        if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0) return new Set(arr)
   265→      } catch {
   266→        // ignore parse errors
   267→      }
   268→    }
   269→    return new Set<string>()
   270→  })
   271→
   272→  // ── Column order state (excludes pinned "name" column) ────────────────
   273→  const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<string[]>(() => {
   274→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY)
   275→    if (stored) {
   276→      try {
   277→        const arr = JSON.parse(stored) as string[]
   278→        if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0) return arr
   279→      } catch {
   280→        // ignore parse errors
   281→      }
   282→    }
   283→    return []
   284→  })
   285→
   286→  // Initialize from defaultVisible when output fields load
   287→  useEffect(() => {
   288→    if (allOutputFields.length > 0 && visibleColumns.size === 0) {
   289→      const defaults = new Set(
   290→        allOutputFields.filter((f) => f.defaultVisible).map((f) => f.name),
   291→      )
   292→      // Always include core fields
   293→      defaults.add('name')
   294→      defaults.add('city')
   295→      defaults.add('state')
   296→      setVisibleColumns(defaults)
   297→    }
   298→  }, [allOutputFields, visibleColumns.size])
   299→
   300→  // Initialize column order when output fields load and no saved order exists
   301→  useEffect(() => {
   302→    if (allOutputFields.length > 0 && columnOrder.length === 0 && visibleColumns.size > 0) {
   303→      // Default order: city, state, distance, myPrice, then other visible fields
   304→      const defaultOrder = ['city', 'state', 'distance', 'myPrice']
   305→      const outputFieldsByName = new Map(allOutputFields.map((f) => [f.name, f]))
   306→      for (const name of visibleColumns) {
   307→        if (name !== 'name' && !defaultOrder.includes(name) && outputFieldsByName.has(name)) {
   308→          defaultOrder.push(name)
   309→        }
   310→      }
   311→      setColumnOrder(defaultOrder)
   312→    }
   313→  }, [allOutputFields, columnOrder.length, visibleColumns])
   314→
   315→  // Persist to localStorage
   316→  useEffect(() => {
   317→    if (visibleColumns.size > 0) {
   318→      localStorage.setItem(VISIBLE_COLUMNS_KEY, JSON.stringify([...visibleColumns]))
   319→    }
   320→  }, [visibleColumns])
   321→
   322→  useEffect(() => {
   323→    if (columnOrder.length > 0) {
   324→      localStorage.setItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY, JSON.stringify(columnOrder))
   325→    }
   326→  }, [columnOrder])
   327→
   328→  const handleToggleColumn = useCallback((fieldName: string) => {
   329→    setVisibleColumns((prev) => {
   330→      const next = new Set(prev)
   331→      if (next.has(fieldName)) {
   332→        next.delete(fieldName)
   333→      } else {
   334→        next.add(fieldName)
   335→      }
   336→      return next
   337→    })
   338→    // Update column order: add to end when toggling on, remove when toggling off
   339→    setColumnOrder((prev) => {
   340→      if (prev.includes(fieldName)) {
   341→        return prev.filter((n) => n !== fieldName)
   342→      }
   343→      return [...prev, fieldName]
   344→    })
   345→  }, [])
   346→
   347→  const handleReorderColumn = useCallback((fieldName: string, direction: 'up' | 'down') => {
   348→    setColumnOrder((prev) => {
   349→      const idx = prev.indexOf(fieldName)
   350→      if (idx === -1) return prev
   351→      const swapIdx = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1
   352→      if (swapIdx < 0 || swapIdx >= prev.length) return prev
   353→      const next = [...prev]
   354→      next[idx] = prev[swapIdx]
   355→      next[swapIdx] = prev[idx]
   356→      return next
   357→    })
   358→  }, [])
   359→
   360→  const handleSetColumnOrder = useCallback((order: string[]) => {
   361→    setColumnOrder(order)
   362→  }, [])
   363→
   364→  // Derive filter state from URL search params + field metadata
   365→  const filters = useMemo(
   366→    () => (fields.length === 0 ? {} : parseUrlToFilters(searchParams, fields)),
   367→    [searchParams, fields],
   368→  )
   369→
   370→  // User location from URL params
   371→  const userLocation = useMemo(() => {
   372→    const lat = searchParams.get('userLat')
   373→    const lng = searchParams.get('userLng')
   374→    if (lat == null || lng == null) return undefined
   375→    const latitude = Number(lat)
   376→    const longitude = Number(lng)
   377→    if (Number.isNaN(latitude) || Number.isNaN(longitude)) return undefined
   378→    const state = searchParams.get('userState') ?? undefined
   379→    return { latitude, longitude, state }
   380→  }, [searchParams])
   381→
   382→  // Sort state from URL params
   383→  const sort = useMemo((): SortState | undefined => {
   384→    const sortParam = searchParams.get('sort')
   385→    if (!sortParam) return undefined
   386→    const dirParam = searchParams.get('sortDir')
   387→    const direction = dirParam === 'DESC' ? ('DESC' as const) : ('ASC' as const)
   388→    return { field: sortParam, direction }
   389→  }, [searchParams])
   390→
   391→  // Ranking blend weights from URL params
   392→  const blendWeights = useMemo(
   393→    () => parseBlendParam(searchParams.get('blend')),
   394→    [searchParams],
   395→  )
   396→  const hasBlend = Object.keys(blendWeights).length > 0
   397→
   398→  // Pagination state from URL params
   399→  const pageSize = useMemo(() => {
   400→    const ps = Number(searchParams.get('pageSize'))
   401→    return PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS.includes(ps) ? ps : DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE
   402→  }, [searchParams])
   403→
   404→  const page = useMemo(() => {
   405→    const p = Number(searchParams.get('page'))
   406→    return p >= 1 ? p : 1
   407→  }, [searchParams])
   408→
   409→  const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize
   410→
   411→  const [searchResult] = useQuery<{
   412→    search: { results: SearchInstitution[]; total: number; limit: number; offset: number }
   413→  }>({
   414→    query: SearchQuery,
   415→    variables: {
   416→      filters: buildFilterInputs(filters),
   417→      limit: pageSize,
   418→      offset,
   419→      sort: hasBlend ? null : (sort ?? null),
   420→      userLocation: userLocation
   421→        ? {
   422→            latitude: userLocation.latitude,
   423→            longitude: userLocation.longitude,
   424→            state: userLocation.state,
   425→          }
   426→        : null,
   427→      rankingBlend: hasBlend
   428→        ? Object.entries(blendWeights).map(([field, weight]) => ({ field, weight }))
   429→        : null,
   430→    },
   431→    pause: fieldsResult.fetching,
   432→  })
   433→
   434→  const themeGroups = useMemo(
   435→    () => fieldsByTheme(fields.filter((f) => f.visibility !== 'HIDDEN')),
   436→    [fields],
   437→  )
   438→
   439→  const institutions = useMemo(
   440→    () => searchResult.data?.search.results ?? [],
   441→    [searchResult.data?.search.results],
   442→  )
   443→  const total = searchResult.data?.search.total ?? 0
   444→  const cappedTotal = Math.min(total, MAX_RESULTS)
   445→  const totalPages = Math.ceil(cappedTotal / pageSize)
   446→
   447→  // "Showing X–Y of Z" display values
   448→  const showStart = cappedTotal === 0 ? 0 : offset + 1
   449→  const showEnd = Math.min(offset + pageSize, cappedTotal)
   450→
   451→  const hasLocation = userLocation != null
   452→  const hasState = userLocation?.state != null
   453→
   454→  function handleFilterChange(fieldName: string, value: unknown | undefined) {
   455→    setSearchParams(
   456→      (prev) => {
   457→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   458→        // Convert legacy ?q= to canonical ?name= so the value is preserved
   459→        const q = next.get('q')
   460→        if (q) {
   461→          next.delete('q')
   462→          if (!next.has('name') && fieldName !== 'name') {
   463→            next.set('name', q)
   464→          }
   465→        }
   466→        if (value === undefined) {
   467→          next.delete(fieldName)
   468→        } else {
   469→          next.set(fieldName, serializeFilterValue(value))
   470→        }
   471→        // Reset to page 1 when filters change
   472→        next.delete('page')
   473→        return next
   474→      },
   475→      { replace: true },
   476→    )
   477→  }
   478→
   479→  const handleLocationChange = useCallback(
   480→    (loc: UserLocationValue | undefined) => {
   481→      setSearchParams(
   482→        (prev) => {
   483→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   484→          if (loc) {
   485→            next.set('userLat', String(loc.latitude))
   486→            next.set('userLng', String(loc.longitude))
   487→            if (loc.state) {
   488→              next.set('userState', loc.state)
   489→            } else {
   490→              next.delete('userState')
   491→            }
   492→          } else {
   493→            next.delete('userLat')
   494→            next.delete('userLng')
   495→            next.delete('userState')
   496→            if (next.get('sort') === 'distance') {
   497→              next.delete('sort')
   498→              next.delete('sortDir')
   499→            }
   500→          }
   501→          next.delete('page')
   502→          return next
   503→        },
   504→        { replace: true },
   505→      )
   506→    },
   507→    [setSearchParams],
   508→  )
   509→
   510→  const handleSort = useCallback(
   511→    (field: string) => {
   512→      setSearchParams(
   513→        (prev) => {
   514→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   515→          const currentField = next.get('sort')
   516→          const currentDir = next.get('sortDir') ?? 'ASC'
   517→
   518→          if (currentField === field) {
   519→            if (currentDir === 'ASC') {
   520→              // ASC → DESC
   521→              next.set('sortDir', 'DESC')
   522→            } else {
   523→              // DESC → clear
   524→              next.delete('sort')
   525→              next.delete('sortDir')
   526→            }
   527→          } else {
   528→            // New field → ASC
   529→            next.set('sort', field)
   530→            next.delete('sortDir')
   531→          }
   532→          next.delete('page')
   533→          return next
   534→        },
   535→        { replace: true },
   536→      )
   537→    },
   538→    [setSearchParams],
   539→  )
   540→
   541→  const handleBlendChange = useCallback(
   542→    (weights: Record<string, number>) => {
   543→      setSearchParams(
   544→        (prev) => {
   545→          const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   546→          const serialized = serializeBlendWeights(weights)
   547→          if (serialized) {
   548→            next.set('blend', serialized)
   549→            // Clear sort when blend is active — blend determines ordering
   550→            next.delete('sort')
   551→            next.delete('sortDir')
   552→          } else {
   553→            next.delete('blend')
   554→          }
   555→          next.delete('page')
   556→          return next
   557→        },
   558→        { replace: true },
   559→      )
   560→    },
   561→    [setSearchParams],
   562→  )
   563→
   564→  function handlePageChange(newPage: number) {
   565→    setSearchParams(
   566→      (prev) => {
   567→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   568→        if (newPage <= 1) {
   569→          next.delete('page')
   570→        } else {
   571→          next.set('page', String(newPage))
   572→        }
   573→        return next
   574→      },
   575→      { replace: true },
   576→    )
   577→  }
   578→
   579→  function handlePageSizeChange(newSize: number) {
   580→    setSearchParams(
   581→      (prev) => {
   582→        const next = new URLSearchParams(prev)
   583→        if (newSize === DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE) {
   584→          next.delete('pageSize')
   585→        } else {
   586→          next.set('pageSize', String(newSize))
   587→        }
   588→        next.delete('page')
   589→        return next
   590→      },
   591→      { replace: true },
   592→    )
   593→  }
   594→
   595→  const handleExportCsv = useCallback(() => {
   596→    const params = new URLSearchParams()
   597→    const filterInputs = buildFilterInputs(filters)
   598→    if (filterInputs.length > 0) {
   599→      params.set('filters', JSON.stringify(filterInputs))
   600→    }
   601→    if (sort) {
   602→      params.set('sort', sort.field)
   603→    }
   604→    if (userLocation) {
   605→      params.set('userLat', String(userLocation.latitude))
   606→      params.set('userLng', String(userLocation.longitude))
   607→      if (userLocation.state) {
   608→        params.set('userState', userLocation.state)
   609→      }
   610→    }
   611→    window.open(`/api/export/csv?${params.toString()}`, '_blank')
   612→  }, [filters, sort, userLocation])
   613→
   614→  const handleExportPdf = useCallback(() => {
   615→    const activeFilters = fields
   616→      .filter((f) => filters[f.name] !== undefined)
   617→      .map((f) => f.label)
   618→    downloadSearchPdf({
   619→      institutions,
   620→      outputFields: allOutputFields,
   621→      activeFilters,
   622→      maxRows: MAX_RESULTS,
   623→      dataSources,
   624→    })
   625→  }, [institutions, allOutputFields, fields, filters, dataSources])
   626→
   627→  const filterContent = (
   628→    <>
   629→      <Title order={6} tt="uppercase" fz="xs" fw={600} lts="0.05em" c="brand.9" mb="md">
   630→        Filters
   631→      </Title>
   632→
   633→      <Box mb="md">
   634→        <Text size="xs" fw={600} tt="uppercase" lts="0.05em" c="dimmed" mb="xs">
   635→          Your Location
   636→        </Text>
   637→        <LocationInput
   638→          value={
   639→            userLocation
   640→              ? {
   641→                  latitude: userLocation.latitude,
   642→                  longitude: userLocation.longitude,
   643→                  state: userLocation.state,
   644→                }
   645→              : undefined
   646→          }
   647→          onChange={handleLocationChange}
   648→        />
   649→      </Box>
   650→
   651→      <Box mb="md">
   652→        <RankingBlendBuilder
   653→          weights={blendWeights}
   654→          onChange={handleBlendChange}
   655→          fields={fields}
   656→        />
   657→      </Box>
   658→
   659→      {fieldsResult.fetching && (
   660→        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed">
   661→          Loading filters…
   662→        </Text>
   663→      )}
   664→      {fieldsResult.error && (
   665→        <Text size="sm" c="red">
   666→          Failed to load filters.
   667→        </Text>
   668→      )}
   669→      {themeGroups.map((group) => (
   670→        <Box key={group.theme} mb="md">
   671→          <Text size="xs" fw={600} tt="uppercase" lts="0.05em" c="dimmed" mb="xs">
   672→            {group.label}
   673→          </Text>
   674→          {group.fields.map((field) => (
   675→            <FilterControl
   676→              key={field.name}
   677→              field={field}
   678→              value={filters[field.name]}
   679→              onChange={(v) => handleFilterChange(field.name, v)}
   680→              dataSources={dataSources}
   681→            />
   682→          ))}
   683→        </Box>
   684→      ))}
   685→    </>
   686→  )
   687→
   688→  return (
   689→    <Box component="main" style={{ display: 'flex', flex: 1, flexDirection: 'column' }}>
   690→      <Container size="xl" px="md" py="md" style={{ display: 'flex', flex: 1 }}>
   691→        <Flex
   692→          direction={{ base: 'column', md: 'row' }}
   693→          gap={0}
   694→          style={{ flex: 1, width: '100%' }}
   695→        >
   696→          {/* Sidebar: on mobile, collapsible; on desktop, fixed-width aside */}
   697→          <Box
   698→            style={{
   699→              ...(isDesktop
   700→                ? {
   701→                    width: 280,
   702→                    flexShrink: 0,
   703→                    overflowY: 'auto',
   704→                    borderRight: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
   705→                    paddingRight: 16,
   706→                  }
   707→                : {}),
   708→            }}
   709→          >
   710→            {!isDesktop && (
   711→              <Box mb="sm">
   712→                <Button
   713→                  variant="default"
   714→                  size="xs"
   715→                  onClick={toggleFilters}
   716→                  leftSection={<SlidersHorizontal size={16} />}
   717→                >
   718→                  {filtersOpened ? 'Hide Filters' : 'Show Filters'}
   719→                </Button>
   720→              </Box>
   721→            )}
   722→
   723→            {isDesktop ? (
   724→              filterContent
   725→            ) : (
   726→              <Collapse in={filtersOpened}>
   727→                <Box
   728→                  mb="md"
   729→                  pb="md"
   730→                  style={{ borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)' }}
   731→                >
   732→                  {filterContent}
   733→                </Box>
   734→              </Collapse>
   735→            )}
   736→          </Box>
   737→
   738→          {/* Results section */}
   739→          <Box style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0 }} pl={{ base: 0, md: 'lg' }}>
   740→            <Group justify="space-between" mb="md" wrap="wrap" gap="sm">
   741→              <Title order={2} c="brand.9">
   742→                Results
   743→              </Title>
   744→              <Group gap="xs" wrap="wrap">
   745→                <ViewToggle value={viewMode} onChange={handleViewModeChange} />
   746→                {hasLocation && (
   747→                  <Button variant="default" size="xs" onClick={() => handleSort('distance')}>
   748→                    {sort?.field === 'distance' ? 'Sorting by distance' : 'Sort by distance'}
   749→                  </Button>
   750→                )}
   751→                {viewMode === 'table' && (
   752→                  <ColumnPicker
   753→                    outputFields={allOutputFields}
   754→                    visibleColumns={visibleColumns}
   755→                    onToggleColumn={handleToggleColumn}
   756→                    columnOrder={columnOrder}
   757→                    onReorderColumn={handleReorderColumn}
   758→                    onSetColumnOrder={handleSetColumnOrder}
   759→                  />
   760→                )}
   761→                <Button
   762→                  variant="default"
   763→                  size="xs"
   764→                  onClick={handleExportPdf}
   765→                  disabled={institutions.length === 0}
   766→                  leftSection={<FileDown size={14} />}
   767→                >
   768→                  Export PDF
   769→                </Button>
   770→                <Button
   771→                  variant="default"
   772→                  size="xs"
   773→                  onClick={handleExportCsv}
   774→                  disabled={institutions.length === 0}
   775→                  leftSection={<Download size={14} />}
   776→                >
   777→                  Export CSV
   778→                </Button>
   779→              </Group>
   780→            </Group>
   781→
   782→            {searchResult.fetching && (
   783→              <div data-testid="results-loading">
   784→                {Array.from({ length: 3 }, (_, i) => (
   785→                  <InstitutionCardSkeleton key={i} />
   786→                ))}
   787→              </div>
   788→            )}
   789→            {searchResult.error && (
   790→              <Stack align="center" py="xl" gap="sm">
   791→                <Text fz="sm" c="red">
   792→                  Something went wrong loading results.
   793→                </Text>
   794→                <Button variant="default" size="xs" onClick={() => window.location.reload()}>
   795→                  Retry
   796→                </Button>
   797→              </Stack>
   798→            )}
   799→            {!searchResult.fetching && searchResult.data && (
   800→              <Stack gap="md">
   801→                <Text fz="sm" c="dimmed" aria-live="polite">
   802→                  {total > MAX_RESULTS
   803→                    ? `Showing ${showStart}–${showEnd} of ${MAX_RESULTS}+ results`
   804→                    : `Showing ${showStart}–${showEnd} of ${total.toLocaleString()} institutions found`}
   805→                </Text>
   806→                {institutions.length === 0 ? (
   807→                  <Stack align="center" py="xl" gap="xs">
   808→                    <Text fw={500}>No colleges match your filters</Text>
   809→                    <Button
   810→                      variant="default"
   811→                      size="xs"
   812→                      style={{ color: 'var(--color-primary)' }}
   813→                      onClick={() => setSearchParams({}, { replace: true })}
   814→                    >
   815→                      Clear all filters
   816→                    </Button>
   817→                  </Stack>
   818→                ) : viewMode === 'card' ? (
   819→                  <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 2, lg: 3 }} spacing="md">
   820→                    {institutions.map((inst) => (
   821→                      <InstitutionCard
   822→                        key={inst.unitID}
   823→                        institution={inst}
   824→                        hasLocation={hasLocation}
   825→                        hasState={hasState}
   826→                        onClick={() => {
   827→                          setSelectedUnitID(inst.unitID)
   828→                          setDetailOpened(true)
   829→                        }}
   830→                      />
   831→                    ))}
   832→                  </SimpleGrid>
   833→                ) : viewMode === 'list' ? (
   834→                  <ResultsList
   835→                    institutions={institutions}
   836→                    hasLocation={hasLocation}
   837→                    hasState={hasState}
   838→                    onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   839→                      setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   840→                      setDetailOpened(true)
   841→                    }}
   842→                  />
   843→                ) : viewMode === 'map' ? (
   844→                  <ResultsMap
   845→                    institutions={institutions}
   846→                    userLocation={userLocation ?? undefined}
   847→                    onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   848→                      setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   849→                      setDetailOpened(true)
   850→                    }}
   851→                  />
   852→                ) : (
   853→                  <Box style={{ overflowX: 'auto' }}>
   854→                    <ResultsTable
   855→                      institutions={institutions}
   856→                      outputFields={allOutputFields}
   857→                      visibleColumns={visibleColumns}
   858→                      columnOrder={columnOrder}
   859→                      onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
   860→                        setSelectedUnitID(unitID)
   861→                        setDetailOpened(true)
   862→                      }}
   863→                      hasLocation={hasLocation}
   864→                      hasState={hasState}
   865→                      hasBlend={hasBlend}
   866→                      sort={sort}
   867→                      onSort={handleSort}
   868→                      dataSources={dataSources}
   869→                    />
   870→                  </Box>
   871→                )}
   872→
   873→                {totalPages > 1 && (
   874→                  <Group gap="md" wrap="wrap" data-testid="pagination-controls">
   875→                    <Pagination total={totalPages} value={page} onChange={handlePageChange} />
   876→                    <Group gap="xs">
   877→                      <NativeSelect
   878→                        id="page-size"
   879→                        aria-label="Per page"
   880→                        value={String(pageSize)}
   881→                        onChange={(e) => handlePageSizeChange(Number(e.target.value))}
   882→                        data={PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS.map((size) => ({
   883→                          value: String(size),
   884→                          label: String(size),
   885→                        }))}
   886→                        size="xs"
   887→                        style={{ width: 70 }}
   888→                      />
   889→                      <Text component="label" htmlFor="page-size" fz="sm" c="dimmed">
   890→                        Per page
   891→                      </Text>
   892→                    </Group>
   893→                  </Group>
   894→                )}
   895→              </Stack>
   896→            )}
   897→          </Box>
   898→        </Flex>
   899→      </Container>
   900→
   901→      <InstitutionDetail
   902→        unitID={selectedUnitID}
   903→        opened={detailOpened}
   904→        onClose={() => setDetailOpened(false)}
   905→      />
   906→    </Box>
   907→  )
   908→}
   909→

1→import { Text, Group, Loader, Center, Card, Badge, SimpleGrid } from '@mantine/core'
     2→import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
     3→import { useQuery } from '@/lib/graphql'
     4→
     5→const SimilarInstitutionsQuery = graphql(`
     6→  query SimilarInstitutions($unitID: String!, $limit: Int) {
     7→    similarInstitutions(unitID: $unitID, limit: $limit) {
     8→      unitID
     9→      name
    10→      city
    11→      state
    12→      distance
    13→      institutionType
    14→      enrollment
    15→      admissionRate
    16→      graduationRate
    17→      inStateTuition
    18→      outOfStateTuition
    19→    }
    20→  }
    21→`)
    22→
    23→interface SimilarInstitution {
    24→  unitID: string
    25→  name: string
    26→  city: string
    27→  state: string
    28→  distance: number
    29→  institutionType: string | null
    30→  enrollment: number | null
    31→  admissionRate: number | null
    32→  graduationRate: number | null
    33→  inStateTuition: number | null
    34→  outOfStateTuition: number | null
    35→}
    36→
    37→interface SimilarInstitutionsData {
    38→  similarInstitutions: SimilarInstitution[]
    39→}
    40→
    41→const INSTITUTION_TYPES: Record<string, string> = {
    42→  '1': 'Public',
    43→  '2': 'Private nonprofit',
    44→  '3': 'Private for-profit',
    45→}
    46→
    47→interface SimilarInstitutionsProps {
    48→  unitID: string
    49→  onSelect?: (unitID: string) => void
    50→}
    51→
    52→export function SimilarInstitutions({ unitID, onSelect }: SimilarInstitutionsProps) {
    53→  const [result] = useQuery<SimilarInstitutionsData>({
    54→    query: SimilarInstitutionsQuery,
    55→    variables: { unitID, limit: 6 },
    56→  })
    57→
    58→  if (result.fetching) {
    59→    return (
    60→      <Center py="md">
    61→        <Loader size="sm" />
    62→      </Center>
    63→    )
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  if (result.error) {
    67→    return (
    68→      <Text c="red" size="sm">
    69→        Failed to load similar institutions.
    70→      </Text>
    71→    )
    72→  }
    73→
    74→  const institutions = result.data?.similarInstitutions ?? []
    75→
    76→  if (institutions.length === 0) {
    77→    return (
    78→      <Text c="dimmed" size="sm">
    79→        No similar institutions found.
    80→      </Text>
    81→    )
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  return (
    85→    <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 2 }} spacing="sm">
    86→      {institutions.map((inst) => (
    87→        <Card
    88→          key={inst.unitID}
    89→          padding="sm"
    90→          radius="sm"
    91→          withBorder
    92→          style={{ cursor: onSelect ? 'pointer' : undefined }}
    93→          onClick={onSelect ? () => onSelect(inst.unitID) : undefined}
    94→        >
    95→          <Text size="sm" fw={600} lineClamp={1}>
    96→            {inst.name}
    97→          </Text>
    98→          <Text size="xs" c="dimmed">
    99→            {inst.city}, {inst.state}
   100→          </Text>
   101→          <Group gap="xs" mt="xs">
   102→            {inst.institutionType && (
   103→              <Badge size="xs" variant="light">
   104→                {INSTITUTION_TYPES[inst.institutionType] ?? inst.institutionType}
   105→              </Badge>
   106→            )}
   107→            {inst.enrollment != null && (
   108→              <Badge size="xs" variant="light" color="gray">
   109→                {inst.enrollment.toLocaleString()} students
   110→              </Badge>
   111→            )}
   112→          </Group>
   113→          <Group gap="md" mt="xs">
   114→            {inst.admissionRate != null && (
   115→              <Text size="xs" c="dimmed">
   116→                Admit: {(inst.admissionRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%
   117→              </Text>
   118→            )}
   119→            {inst.graduationRate != null && (
   120→              <Text size="xs" c="dimmed">
   121→                Grad: {(inst.graduationRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%
   122→              </Text>
   123→            )}
   124→            {inst.inStateTuition != null && (
   125→              <Text size="xs" c="dimmed">
   126→                Tuition: ${inst.inStateTuition.toLocaleString()}
   127→              </Text>
   128→            )}
   129→          </Group>
   130→        </Card>
   131→      ))}
   132→    </SimpleGrid>
   133→  )
   134→}
   135→

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Fix and Enhance the Institution Detail Card

### Problem Summary

The `InstitutionDetail` modal shows "no data" because it fires a separate GraphQL query (`institution(unitID)`) that uses `BuildInstitutionQuery()` in `api/internal/search/sql.go`. That function constructs SQL with flat JSONB paths (`data->>'control'`) when the actual data lives under nested paths (`data->'nces'->>'ownership'`). Meanwhile, the main search query already fetches and correctly parses the full `data` JSONB column via `extract*` functions in `search_data_extract.go`.

### Chosen Approach: Pass Search Result Data Directly

Rather than fixing the broken SQL paths in `BuildInstitutionQuery`, eliminate the separate query entirely. The search results already contain all the data the detail card needs (and more). This removes a network round-trip, eliminates duplicated JSONB path knowledge, and is more maintainable.

---

### Step 1: Change InstitutionDetail Props (frontend-only change)

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

Current interface:
```ts
interface InstitutionDetailProps {
  unitID: string | null
  opened: boolean
  onClose: () => void
}
```

New interface:
```ts
interface InstitutionDetailProps {
  institution: SearchInstitution | null
  opened: boolean
  onClose: () => void
}
```

Changes:
- Remove the `InstitutionDetailQuery` graphql query definition entirely
- Remove the `InstitutionDetailData` interface
- Remove the `useQuery` call inside the component
- Import `SearchInstitution` from `./types`
- Accept the full `SearchInstitution` object as a prop
- Remove the `INSTITUTION_TYPES` mapping (ownership is already a string like "1", "2", "3" -- but now it comes from `academics.institutionType` which is the raw value from `nces.ownership`). Keep the mapping but source from `institution.academics?.institutionType`.
- Remove all loading/error states (data is already available)
- Keep the "Institution not found" state for when `institution` is null

### Step 2: Rewrite the Detail Card Display

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

Replace the current three-group layout (Academics/Cost/Outcomes) with a richer display using all themed data from `SearchInstitution`:

**Section layout (two-column SimpleGrid):**

1. **Academics** -- from `institution.academics`
   - Type (institutionType with INSTITUTION_TYPES mapping)
   - Highest Degree
   - Enrollment
   - Graduate Enrollment
   - Student-Faculty Ratio (formatted as "N:1")
   - Full-Time Faculty Rate (percentage)
   - Gender (coed/men-only/women-only)
   - Online Only (Yes/No)

2. **Cost** -- from `institution.cost`
   - In-State Tuition (currency)
   - Out-of-State Tuition (currency)
   - Avg Net Price (currency)
   - Room & Board (currency)
   - Avg Net Price (Low Income) (currency)
   - Pell Grant Rate (percentage)
   - Federal Loan Rate (percentage)
   - Endowment (currency)

3. **Outcomes** -- from `institution.outcomes`
   - Admission Rate (percentage)
   - Graduation Rate (percentage)
   - Retention Rate (percentage)
   - Transfer Rate (percentage)

4. **Rankings** -- from `institution.rankings`
   - Only show rankings that have values (non-null)
   - US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly (National and Liberal Arts), Niche, Money Score

5. **Location** -- from `institution.locationDetails`
   - Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance

6. **Admissions** -- from `institution.admissions`
   - Application Platforms (comma-joined list)

7. **Athletics** -- from `institution.athletics`
   - NCAA Division, NCAA Conference

8. **Climate** -- from `institution.climate`
   - Koppen Climate, Temperature, Precipitation

9. **Culture** -- from `institution.culture`
   - Partisan Direction, Partisan Score

**Only render a section if its data object is non-null and has at least one non-null field.** This keeps the card clean for institutions with sparse data.

Keep the existing `DetailRow` component and formatting functions (`formatPercent`, `formatCurrency`, `formatNumber`). Add a `formatBoolean` helper for Yes/No display.

### Step 3: Update Search.tsx to Pass Institution Object

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/pages/Search.tsx`

Current state management:
```ts
const [selectedUnitID, setSelectedUnitID] = useState<string | null>(null)
```

Change to:
```ts
const [selectedInstitution, setSelectedInstitution] = useState<SearchInstitution | null>(null)
```

Update all click handlers that currently do:
```ts
setSelectedUnitID(inst.unitID)
setDetailOpened(true)
```

To instead do:
```ts
setSelectedInstitution(inst)
setDetailOpened(true)
```

For `ResultsList`, `ResultsMap`, and `ResultsTable` which pass `unitID` via `onClickInstitution`, change the callback signature from `(unitID: string) => void` to pass the full institution. This requires updating:

**Option A (minimal change):** Keep the `onClickInstitution` callbacks as `(unitID: string) => void`, and look up the institution from the `institutions` array in the callback:
```ts
onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
  const inst = institutions.find(i => i.unitID === unitID)
  setSelectedInstitution(inst ?? null)
  setDetailOpened(true)
}}
```

This is the better option because it avoids changing the prop types of `ResultsList`, `ResultsMap`, and `ResultsTable`.

Update the JSX:
```tsx
<InstitutionDetail
  institution={selectedInstitution}
  opened={detailOpened}
  onClose={() => setDetailOpened(false)}
/>
```

Remove the `selectedUnitID` state variable entirely.

### Step 4: Update SimilarInstitutions Integration

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

The `SimilarInstitutions` component uses `unitID` as a prop. Since we now have the full institution object, pass `institution.unitID` to `SimilarInstitutions`:
```tsx
<SimilarInstitutions unitID={institution.unitID} />
```

The `SimilarInstitutions` component itself makes its own GraphQL query (`similarInstitutions`) -- this is a different resolver and can remain as-is for now. Its data requirements are different (it needs similarity-computed data like distance between institutions).

### Step 5: Update Tests

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx`

Major test changes:
- Remove all `mockUseQuery` setup -- the component no longer makes GraphQL queries
- Still need to mock `useMediaQuery` from `@mantine/hooks`
- Need to mock `useQuery` for the `SimilarInstitutions` child component (it still queries)
- Change `MOCK_INSTITUTION` to match `SearchInstitution` shape (with nested `academics`, `cost`, `outcomes` etc.)
- Update `renderDetail` to pass `institution` prop instead of `unitID`
- Remove the "shows a loader while fetching" test (no longer applicable)
- Remove the "shows an error message on query failure" test (no longer applicable)
- Remove the "pauses the query when unitID is null" test (no longer applicable)
- Keep/update "renders institution details when data is loaded" with new mock shape
- Keep/update "shows Institution not found when institution is null"
- Keep/update "renders dashes for null optional fields"
- Add tests for new sections: rankings display, athletics, climate, culture, location

New mock shape:
```ts
const MOCK_INSTITUTION: SearchInstitution = {
  unitID: '166683',
  name: 'MIT',
  city: 'Cambridge',
  state: 'MA',
  latitude: 42.36,
  longitude: -71.09,
  academics: {
    institutionType: '2',
    highestDegree: '4',
    enrollment: 11574,
    gradEnrollment: 6990,
    studentFacultyRatio: 3,
    fullTimeFacultyRate: 0.92,
    gender: 'coed',
    onlineOnly: false,
  },
  cost: {
    tuitionInState: 57590,
    tuitionOutOfState: 57590,
    avgNetPrice: 21642,
    roomAndBoard: 17810,
    avgNetPriceLowIncome: 4800,
    pellGrantRate: 0.18,
    federalLoanRate: 0.15,
    endowment: 27400000000,
  },
  outcomes: {
    admissionRate: 0.04,
    graduationRate: 0.94,
    retentionRate: 0.99,
    transferRate: 0.01,
  },
  rankings: {
    usNewsNational: 1,
    forbes: 3,
    niche: 2,
    usNewsLiberalArts: null,
    washingtonMonthlyNational: 5,
    washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts: null,
    moneyScore: 4.5,
  },
}
```

### Step 6: Backend Cleanup (Optional, Recommended)

**Files:**
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/internal/search/sql.go` -- remove `BuildInstitutionQuery()` function
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go` -- remove the `Institution` resolver method
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/schema/search.graphql` -- remove `InstitutionDetail` type and `institution(unitID)` query from `extend type Query`

After removing the schema entries, run:
```bash
make api-generate
make web-schema-sync
```

This removes dead code. The `InstitutionDetail` GraphQL type and `institution` query are no longer needed since the frontend won't call them.

**Note:** The `SimilarInstitutions` component and its `similarInstitutions` query in `similarity.graphql` / `similarity.resolvers.go` remain untouched -- they serve a different purpose.

### Step 7: Verification

1. `make web-build` -- TypeScript compilation + Vite build must pass with zero errors
2. `pnpm lint` in `web/` -- zero warnings
3. `pnpm test` in `web/` -- all tests pass
4. `make api-test-unit` -- Go unit tests pass (if backend cleanup done)
5. Manual verification: run `make dev`, perform a search, click a result card, verify the detail modal shows rich data across all themed sections

---

### Sequencing and Dependencies

| Step | Depends on | Files touched |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| 1. Change InstitutionDetail props | None | InstitutionDetail.tsx |
| 2. Rewrite detail card display | Step 1 | InstitutionDetail.tsx |
| 3. Update Search.tsx | Step 1 | Search.tsx |
| 4. Update SimilarInstitutions usage | Step 1 | InstitutionDetail.tsx (minor) |
| 5. Update tests | Steps 1-3 | InstitutionDetail.test.tsx |
| 6. Backend cleanup | Steps 1-3 verified | sql.go, search.resolvers.go, search.graphql, then make api-generate + web-schema-sync |
| 7. Verification | All above | None (read-only commands) |

Steps 1-4 can be done in a single pass on InstitutionDetail.tsx + Search.tsx. Step 5 follows immediately. Step 6 is a separate commit.

### Potential Challenges

1. **Type compatibility**: The `SearchInstitution` type from gql.tada may differ slightly from the manually-defined `SearchInstitution` interface in `types.ts`. Need to verify they align, or use `ResultOf<typeof SearchQuery>` to derive the type.

2. **SimilarInstitutions mock in tests**: Since `InstitutionDetail` renders `SimilarInstitutions` (which still uses `useQuery`), the test must still mock `urql`'s `useQuery` for the child component. The mock should return empty data for the `SimilarInstitutions` query.

3. **Stale data concern**: If a user leaves the search page open for a long time, the data passed to the detail card could be stale. This is acceptable because (a) the same concern exists with the search results themselves, and (b) college data changes infrequently.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx` - Primary file to rewrite: remove GraphQL query, accept data prop, display all themed sections
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/pages/Search.tsx` - Update state management to pass full institution object instead of unitID
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx` - Rewrite tests for new prop-based interface
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/types.ts` - Reference for SearchInstitution type (no changes needed)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/schema/search.graphql` - Backend cleanup: remove InstitutionDetail type and institution query

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for fixing and enhancing the institution detail card in the Kapow codebase (worktree at /home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card).

## Background

The detail card (InstitutionDetail.tsx) is a modal that pops up when clicking a search result card. It currently shows "no data" because the backend query uses wrong JSONB paths.

## Root Cause of the Bug

The `BuildInstitutionQuery()` in `api/internal/search/sql.go` (line 159-171) uses incorrect JSONB paths. The `data` column in `dataset_merged` is a nested JSONB object where most fields live under `data->'nces'->>'key_name'`, but the query uses flat paths like `data->>'control'` instead of `data #>> '{nces,ownership}'`.

Wrong paths in current code:
- `data->>'control'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,ownership}'`
- `data->>'enrollment'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,size}'`
- `data->>'graduation_rate'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,completion_rate_4yr_150nt}'`
- `data->>'admission_rate'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,admission_rate_overall}'`
- `data->>'retention_rate'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,retention_rate_four_year_full_time_pooled}'`
- `data->>'student_faculty_ratio'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,demographics_student_faculty_ratio}'`
- `data->>'tuition_in_state'` → should be `data #>> '{nces,tuition_in_state}'` (key name is correct but path needs nces prefix)
- Same for tuition_out_of_state and avg_net_price

## Key Architectural Insight

The search query already fetches the full `data` JSONB column and parses it via `extract*` functions in `search_data_extract.go`. These extract functions correctly navigate the nested JSONB structure (e.g., `jsonbObject(data, "nces")` then `jsonbString(nces, "ownership")`). The detail card makes a SEPARATE query with broken paths.

## Proposed Approach: Eliminate the Separate Query

Instead of fixing the broken SQL paths, **pass the already-fetched search result data** directly to the detail modal. The search results already contain all the data the detail card needs (and more). This:
1. Eliminates a network round-trip
2. Eliminates duplicated JSONB path knowledge
3. Is simpler and more maintainable

### Implementation Steps

**Frontend changes:**

1. **Search.tsx**: Instead of passing just `unitID` to InstitutionDetail, pass the full `SearchInstitution` object
2. **InstitutionDetail.tsx**: 
   - Accept the full institution data as a prop instead of fetching via separate query
   - Remove the separate GraphQL query
   - Rewrite the card to display themed field groups using the nested data types (academics, cost, outcomes, etc.)

**For the enhancement (themed field groups):**

The current search result GraphQL types already include:
- academics: institutionType, highestDegree, enrollment, gradEnrollment, studentFacultyRatio, fullTimeFacultyRate, gender, onlineOnly
- cost: avgNetPrice, tuitionInState, tuitionOutOfState, roomAndBoard, avgNetPriceLowIncome, pellGrantRate, federalLoanRate, endowment
- outcomes: admissionRate, graduationRate, retentionRate, transferRate
- rankings: usNewsNational, usNewsLiberalArts, forbes, washingtonMonthlyNational, washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts, niche, moneyScore
- admissions: applicationPlatforms
- athletics: ncaaDivision, ncaaConference
- climate: koppenClimate, koppenTemperature, koppenPrecipitation
- culture: partisanDirection, partisanScore
- locationDetails: locale, region, metroArea, metroDistance

Fields MISSING from current GraphQL types but requested in the enhancement:
- SAT range, ACT range, application fee (admissions theme)
- Carnegie Basic classification
- Median earnings after graduation
- Minority-serving designations (HBCU, HSI, AANAPISI, PBI)
- Religious affiliation
- School URL, accreditor
- Coeducational status (can be derived from gender field)

**For missing fields**, we need to:
1. Check if they exist in the search registry (registry.go) to find their JSONB paths
2. Add them to GraphQL schema types (search.graphql)
3. Add extract logic in search_data_extract.go
4. Update the frontend query in Search.tsx to request them
5. Add them to web/schema.graphql via make web-schema-sync

## Questions to Consider

1. Should we add ALL the missing fields from the enhancement spec, or just display what's already available in the search results?
2. The detail card currently also has a "Similar Schools" feature - should we keep that?
3. Carnegie Basic needs a code→label mapping. Should this live in the frontend or backend?

## Files to Modify

**Backend (only if adding new fields):**
- `api/graph/schema/search.graphql` - Add new fields to sub-types
- `api/graph/resolver/search_data_extract.go` - Add extract logic for new fields
- `api/internal/search/sql.go` - Can remove `BuildInstitutionQuery` if no longer needed
- `api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go` - Can remove `Institution` resolver if no longer needed

**Frontend:**
- `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` - Pass full institution data to detail modal
- `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx` - Complete rewrite to accept data as prop and display themed groups
- `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx` - Update tests for new component interface
- `web/src/components/search/types.ts` - May need updates if adding new fields
- `web/schema.graphql` + `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` - Regenerated via make web-schema-sync

Please produce a detailed step-by-step implementation plan.

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Fix and Enhance the Institution Detail Card

### Problem Summary

The `InstitutionDetail` modal shows "no data" because it fires a separate GraphQL query (`institution(unitID)`) that uses `BuildInstitutionQuery()` in `api/internal/search/sql.go`. That function constructs SQL with flat JSONB paths (`data->>'control'`) when the actual data lives under nested paths (`data->'nces'->>'ownership'`). Meanwhile, the main search query already fetches and correctly parses the full `data` JSONB column via `extract*` functions in `search_data_extract.go`.

### Chosen Approach: Pass Search Result Data Directly

Rather than fixing the broken SQL paths in `BuildInstitutionQuery`, eliminate the separate query entirely. The search results already contain all the data the detail card needs (and more). This removes a network round-trip, eliminates duplicated JSONB path knowledge, and is more maintainable.

---

### Step 1: Change InstitutionDetail Props (frontend-only change)

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

Current interface:
```ts
interface InstitutionDetailProps {
  unitID: string | null
  opened: boolean
  onClose: () => void
}
```

New interface:
```ts
interface InstitutionDetailProps {
  institution: SearchInstitution | null
  opened: boolean
  onClose: () => void
}
```

Changes:
- Remove the `InstitutionDetailQuery` graphql query definition entirely
- Remove the `InstitutionDetailData` interface
- Remove the `useQuery` call inside the component
- Import `SearchInstitution` from `./types`
- Accept the full `SearchInstitution` object as a prop
- Remove the `INSTITUTION_TYPES` mapping (ownership is already a string like "1", "2", "3" -- but now it comes from `academics.institutionType` which is the raw value from `nces.ownership`). Keep the mapping but source from `institution.academics?.institutionType`.
- Remove all loading/error states (data is already available)
- Keep the "Institution not found" state for when `institution` is null

### Step 2: Rewrite the Detail Card Display

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

Replace the current three-group layout (Academics/Cost/Outcomes) with a richer display using all themed data from `SearchInstitution`:

**Section layout (two-column SimpleGrid):**

1. **Academics** -- from `institution.academics`
   - Type (institutionType with INSTITUTION_TYPES mapping)
   - Highest Degree
   - Enrollment
   - Graduate Enrollment
   - Student-Faculty Ratio (formatted as "N:1")
   - Full-Time Faculty Rate (percentage)
   - Gender (coed/men-only/women-only)
   - Online Only (Yes/No)

2. **Cost** -- from `institution.cost`
   - In-State Tuition (currency)
   - Out-of-State Tuition (currency)
   - Avg Net Price (currency)
   - Room & Board (currency)
   - Avg Net Price (Low Income) (currency)
   - Pell Grant Rate (percentage)
   - Federal Loan Rate (percentage)
   - Endowment (currency)

3. **Outcomes** -- from `institution.outcomes`
   - Admission Rate (percentage)
   - Graduation Rate (percentage)
   - Retention Rate (percentage)
   - Transfer Rate (percentage)

4. **Rankings** -- from `institution.rankings`
   - Only show rankings that have values (non-null)
   - US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly (National and Liberal Arts), Niche, Money Score

5. **Location** -- from `institution.locationDetails`
   - Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance

6. **Admissions** -- from `institution.admissions`
   - Application Platforms (comma-joined list)

7. **Athletics** -- from `institution.athletics`
   - NCAA Division, NCAA Conference

8. **Climate** -- from `institution.climate`
   - Koppen Climate, Temperature, Precipitation

9. **Culture** -- from `institution.culture`
   - Partisan Direction, Partisan Score

**Only render a section if its data object is non-null and has at least one non-null field.** This keeps the card clean for institutions with sparse data.

Keep the existing `DetailRow` component and formatting functions (`formatPercent`, `formatCurrency`, `formatNumber`). Add a `formatBoolean` helper for Yes/No display.

### Step 3: Update Search.tsx to Pass Institution Object

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/pages/Search.tsx`

Current state management:
```ts
const [selectedUnitID, setSelectedUnitID] = useState<string | null>(null)
```

Change to:
```ts
const [selectedInstitution, setSelectedInstitution] = useState<SearchInstitution | null>(null)
```

Update all click handlers that currently do:
```ts
setSelectedUnitID(inst.unitID)
setDetailOpened(true)
```

To instead do:
```ts
setSelectedInstitution(inst)
setDetailOpened(true)
```

For `ResultsList`, `ResultsMap`, and `ResultsTable` which pass `unitID` via `onClickInstitution`, change the callback signature from `(unitID: string) => void` to pass the full institution. This requires updating:

**Option A (minimal change):** Keep the `onClickInstitution` callbacks as `(unitID: string) => void`, and look up the institution from the `institutions` array in the callback:
```ts
onClickInstitution={(unitID) => {
  const inst = institutions.find(i => i.unitID === unitID)
  setSelectedInstitution(inst ?? null)
  setDetailOpened(true)
}}
```

This is the better option because it avoids changing the prop types of `ResultsList`, `ResultsMap`, and `ResultsTable`.

Update the JSX:
```tsx
<InstitutionDetail
  institution={selectedInstitution}
  opened={detailOpened}
  onClose={() => setDetailOpened(false)}
/>
```

Remove the `selectedUnitID` state variable entirely.

### Step 4: Update SimilarInstitutions Integration

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

The `SimilarInstitutions` component uses `unitID` as a prop. Since we now have the full institution object, pass `institution.unitID` to `SimilarInstitutions`:
```tsx
<SimilarInstitutions unitID={institution.unitID} />
```

The `SimilarInstitutions` component itself makes its own GraphQL query (`similarInstitutions`) -- this is a different resolver and can remain as-is for now. Its data requirements are different (it needs similarity-computed data like distance between institutions).

### Step 5: Update Tests

**File:** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx`

Major test changes:
- Remove all `mockUseQuery` setup -- the component no longer makes GraphQL queries
- Still need to mock `useMediaQuery` from `@mantine/hooks`
- Need to mock `useQuery` for the `SimilarInstitutions` child component (it still queries)
- Change `MOCK_INSTITUTION` to match `SearchInstitution` shape (with nested `academics`, `cost`, `outcomes` etc.)
- Update `renderDetail` to pass `institution` prop instead of `unitID`
- Remove the "shows a loader while fetching" test (no longer applicable)
- Remove the "shows an error message on query failure" test (no longer applicable)
- Remove the "pauses the query when unitID is null" test (no longer applicable)
- Keep/update "renders institution details when data is loaded" with new mock shape
- Keep/update "shows Institution not found when institution is null"
- Keep/update "renders dashes for null optional fields"
- Add tests for new sections: rankings display, athletics, climate, culture, location

New mock shape:
```ts
const MOCK_INSTITUTION: SearchInstitution = {
  unitID: '166683',
  name: 'MIT',
  city: 'Cambridge',
  state: 'MA',
  latitude: 42.36,
  longitude: -71.09,
  academics: {
    institutionType: '2',
    highestDegree: '4',
    enrollment: 11574,
    gradEnrollment: 6990,
    studentFacultyRatio: 3,
    fullTimeFacultyRate: 0.92,
    gender: 'coed',
    onlineOnly: false,
  },
  cost: {
    tuitionInState: 57590,
    tuitionOutOfState: 57590,
    avgNetPrice: 21642,
    roomAndBoard: 17810,
    avgNetPriceLowIncome: 4800,
    pellGrantRate: 0.18,
    federalLoanRate: 0.15,
    endowment: 27400000000,
  },
  outcomes: {
    admissionRate: 0.04,
    graduationRate: 0.94,
    retentionRate: 0.99,
    transferRate: 0.01,
  },
  rankings: {
    usNewsNational: 1,
    forbes: 3,
    niche: 2,
    usNewsLiberalArts: null,
    washingtonMonthlyNational: 5,
    washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts: null,
    moneyScore: 4.5,
  },
}
```

### Step 6: Backend Cleanup (Optional, Recommended)

**Files:**
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/internal/search/sql.go` -- remove `BuildInstitutionQuery()` function
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go` -- remove the `Institution` resolver method
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/schema/search.graphql` -- remove `InstitutionDetail` type and `institution(unitID)` query from `extend type Query`

After removing the schema entries, run:
```bash
make api-generate
make web-schema-sync
```

This removes dead code. The `InstitutionDetail` GraphQL type and `institution` query are no longer needed since the frontend won't call them.

**Note:** The `SimilarInstitutions` component and its `similarInstitutions` query in `similarity.graphql` / `similarity.resolvers.go` remain untouched -- they serve a different purpose.

### Step 7: Verification

1. `make web-build` -- TypeScript compilation + Vite build must pass with zero errors
2. `pnpm lint` in `web/` -- zero warnings
3. `pnpm test` in `web/` -- all tests pass
4. `make api-test-unit` -- Go unit tests pass (if backend cleanup done)
5. Manual verification: run `make dev`, perform a search, click a result card, verify the detail modal shows rich data across all themed sections

---

### Sequencing and Dependencies

| Step | Depends on | Files touched |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| 1. Change InstitutionDetail props | None | InstitutionDetail.tsx |
| 2. Rewrite detail card display | Step 1 | InstitutionDetail.tsx |
| 3. Update Search.tsx | Step 1 | Search.tsx |
| 4. Update SimilarInstitutions usage | Step 1 | InstitutionDetail.tsx (minor) |
| 5. Update tests | Steps 1-3 | InstitutionDetail.test.tsx |
| 6. Backend cleanup | Steps 1-3 verified | sql.go, search.resolvers.go, search.graphql, then make api-generate + web-schema-sync |
| 7. Verification | All above | None (read-only commands) |

Steps 1-4 can be done in a single pass on InstitutionDetail.tsx + Search.tsx. Step 5 follows immediately. Step 6 is a separate commit.

### Potential Challenges

1. **Type compatibility**: The `SearchInstitution` type from gql.tada may differ slightly from the manually-defined `SearchInstitution` interface in `types.ts`. Need to verify they align, or use `ResultOf<typeof SearchQuery>` to derive the type.

2. **SimilarInstitutions mock in tests**: Since `InstitutionDetail` renders `SimilarInstitutions` (which still uses `useQuery`), the test must still mock `urql`'s `useQuery` for the child component. The mock should return empty data for the `SimilarInstitutions` query.

3. **Stale data concern**: If a user leaves the search page open for a long time, the data passed to the detail card could be stale. This is acceptable because (a) the same concern exists with the search results themselves, and (b) college data changes infrequently.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx` - Primary file to rewrite: remove GraphQL query, accept data prop, display all themed sections
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/pages/Search.tsx` - Update state management to pass full institution object instead of unitID
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx` - Rewrite tests for new prop-based interface
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/web/src/components/search/types.ts` - Reference for SearchInstitution type (no changes needed)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/worktree/fix-detail-card/api/graph/schema/search.graphql` - Backend cleanup: remove InstitutionDetail type and institution query

export type FieldVisibility = 'VISIBLE' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'HIDDEN'
export type FieldSearchability = 'SEARCHABLE' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'NOT_SEARCHABLE'

export type Theme =
  | 'CORE'
  | 'LOCATION'
  | 'ACADEMICS'
  | 'COST'
  | 'OUTCOMES'
  | 'RANKING'
  | 'ADMISSIONS'
  | 'ATHLETICS'
  | 'FIELDS_OF_STUDY'
  | 'CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT'
  | 'WEATHER'
  | 'PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT'
  | 'DEMOGRAPHICS'
  | 'REPUTATION'
  | 'SAFETY'
  | 'STUDENT_LIFE'
  | 'METADATA'
  | 'OTHER'

export interface SearchFieldOption {
  value: string
  displayName: string
}

export interface SearchField {
  __typename: string
  name: string
  label: string
  description: string
  theme: Theme
  visibility: FieldVisibility
  searchability: FieldSearchability
  exportable: boolean
  dataSourceID?: string | null
  // ChoiceSearchField only — present when __typename === 'ChoiceSearchField'
  options?: SearchFieldOption[]
  minSelections?: number
  maxSelections?: number | null
  // NumericalSearchField / NumericalRangeSearchField metadata
  min?: number | null
  max?: number | null
  step?: number | null
  unit?: SearchFieldUnit | null
  defaultValue?: number | null
  defaultLower?: number | null
  defaultUpper?: number | null
  // GeoSearchField metadata
  defaultLatitude?: number | null
  defaultLongitude?: number | null
  maxRadiusMiles?: number | null
}

export interface InstitutionLocation {
  locale?: string | null
  region?: string | null
  metroArea?: string | null
  metroDistance?: number | null
}

export interface InstitutionAcademics {
  institutionType?: string | null
  highestDegree?: string | null
  enrollment?: number | null
  gradEnrollment?: number | null
  studentFacultyRatio?: number | null
  fullTimeFacultyRate?: number | null
  gender?: string | null
  onlineOnly?: boolean | null
}

export interface InstitutionCost {
  avgNetPrice?: number | null
  tuitionInState?: number | null
  tuitionOutOfState?: number | null
  roomAndBoard?: number | null
  avgNetPriceLowIncome?: number | null
  pellGrantRate?: number | null
  federalLoanRate?: number | null
  endowment?: number | null
}

export interface InstitutionOutcomes {
  admissionRate?: number | null
  graduationRate?: number | null
  retentionRate?: number | null
  transferRate?: number | null
}

export interface InstitutionRankings {
  usNewsNational?: number | null
  usNewsLiberalArts?: number | null
  forbes?: number | null
  washingtonMonthlyNational?: number | null
  washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts?: number | null
  niche?: number | null
  moneyScore?: number | null
}

export interface InstitutionAdmissions {
  applicationPlatforms?: string[] | null
}

export interface InstitutionAthletics {
  ncaaDivision?: string | null
  ncaaConference?: string | null
}

export interface InstitutionClimate {
  koppenClimate?: string | null
  koppenTemperature?: string | null
  koppenPrecipitation?: string | null
}

export interface InstitutionCulture {
  partisanDirection?: string | null
  partisanScore?: number | null
}

export interface SearchInstitution {
  unitID: string
  name: string
  city: string
  state: string
  latitude?: number | null
  longitude?: number | null
  distance?: number | null
  myPrice?: number | null
  blendScore?: number | null
  locationDetails?: InstitutionLocation | null
  academics?: InstitutionAcademics | null
  cost?: InstitutionCost | null
  outcomes?: InstitutionOutcomes | null
  rankings?: InstitutionRankings | null
  admissions?: InstitutionAdmissions | null
  athletics?: InstitutionAthletics | null
  climate?: InstitutionClimate | null
  culture?: InstitutionCulture | null
}

export type SearchFieldUnit = 'DOLLARS' | 'PERCENTAGE' | 'MILES' | 'HOURS' | 'MINUTES'

export interface SearchOutputField {
  name: string
  label: string
  description: string
  theme: Theme
  visibility: FieldVisibility
  exportable: boolean
  dataSourceID?: string | null
  defaultVisible: boolean
  sortable: boolean
  unit?: SearchFieldUnit | null
  outputKey?: string | null
}

- SAT range - NOT in registry

- Application fee - NOT in registry
- Median earnings - NOT in registry
- Minority-serving designations (HBCU, HSI, AANAPISI, PBI) - NOT in registry
- Religious affiliation - NOT in registry
- School URL - NOT in registry
- Accreditor - NOT in registry
- Carnegie size/setting - NOT in registry


- Average class size - NOT in registry

Many of these might actually exist in the NCES data stored in the JSONB, so I need to check if they're present in the fixture data. My approach should be to first fix the search bug, then display all the existing fields in organized groups, and finally add any missing fields to the GraphQL schema and extract functions if the underlying data is available.

For fields that genuinely don't exist in the data—like "Physical Environment" and "Safety" that the issue explicitly mentions skipping—I'll leave those out entirely. Now I have a clear enough picture to write out the final plan.

input.content:
# Fix Detail Card: No Data Bug + Enhanced Field Display

## Context

The institution detail card (expanded result card) shows no data when clicked. The root cause is `BuildInstitutionQuery()` in `api/internal/search/sql.go:159` which uses incorrect JSONB paths (e.g., `data->>'control'` instead of `data #>> '{nces,ownership}'`). Meanwhile, the main search query already fetches the full `data` JSONB and correctly extracts all fields via `extract*` functions in `search_data_extract.go`.

**Fix**: Eliminate the broken separate query. Pass the already-fetched search result data directly to the detail modal. Then enhance the display with themed field groups.

---

## Step 1: Fix the Bug — Pass Search Data to Detail Modal

### 1a. Update InstitutionDetail props

**File**: `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

- Change props from `{ unitID: string | null, opened, onClose }` to `{ institution: SearchInstitution | null, opened, onClose }`
- Import `SearchInstitution` from `./types`
- Remove `InstitutionDetailQuery` (graphql query), `InstitutionDetailData` interface, and `useQuery` call
- Remove loading/error states (data is already available from search results)
- Keep the "Institution not found" state for null institution

### 1b. Update Search.tsx state management

**File**: `web/src/pages/Search.tsx`

- Replace `selectedUnitID: string | null` state with `selectedInstitution: SearchInstitution | null`
- In click handlers, find institution from `institutions` array by unitID and set it
- Pass `institution={selectedInstitution}` to `<InstitutionDetail>` instead of `unitID`

---

## Step 2: Add Missing Fields to GraphQL Schema + Backend

Several fields in the issue spec exist in the NCES JSONB data but aren't in current GraphQL types. Add to the appropriate sub-types:

### 2a. Schema additions (`api/graph/schema/search.graphql`)

**InstitutionAcademics** — add:
- `carnegieBasic: Int` (nces.carnegie_basic)
- `carnegieSize: String` (nces.carnegie_size_setting — the locale/setting code)

**InstitutionCost** — add:
- `totalPrice: Int` (nces.total_price_for_in_state or out_of_state)

**InstitutionAdmissions** — add:
- `satMath25: Int`, `satMath75: Int` (nces.sat_scores_midpoint_math_25th/75th)
- `satVerbal25: Int`, `satVerbal75: Int` (nces.sat_scores_midpoint_critical_reading_25th/75th)
- `actComposite25: Int`, `actComposite75: Int` (nces.act_scores_25th/75th percentile)
- `applicationFee: Int` (nces.application_fee)

**InstitutionOutcomes** — add:
- `medianEarnings: Int` (nces.earnings_median_after_10_years or similar)

**InstitutionDemographics** (NEW type):
- `totalEnrollment: Int` (same as academics.enrollment, but semantically fits here)
- `undergradEnrollment: Int` (nces.undergrad_enrollment)
- `percentMale: Float`, `percentFemale: Float`
- `hbcu: Boolean`, `hsi: Boolean`, `aanapisi: Boolean`, `pbi: Boolean`

**InstitutionMetadata** (NEW type):
- `schoolUrl: String` (nces.insturl)
- `accreditor: String` (nces.accreditor)
- `highestDegree: String` (same as academics.highestDegree)
- `religiousAffiliation: String` (nces.religious_affiliation)

**SearchInstitution** — add:
- `demographics: InstitutionDemographics`
- `metadata: InstitutionMetadata`

### 2b. Backend extract functions (`api/graph/resolver/search_data_extract.go`)

- Add `extractDemographics(data)` and `extractMetadata(data)` functions
- Extend `extractAdmissions`, `extractCost`, `extractOutcomes` with new fields
- Add carnegie fields to `extractAcademics`

### 2c. Wire new extractors (`api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go`)

Add `inst.Demographics = extractDemographics(rawData)` and `inst.Metadata = extractMetadata(rawData)` in the search results scan loop (line ~142-152).

### 2d. Regenerate

```bash
make api-generate
make web-schema-sync
```

**NOTE**: Only add fields that actually exist in the NCES fixture data. Check `data` JSONB keys before adding. If a key doesn't exist, skip it — the field will just return null.

---

## Step 3: Enhance the Detail Card Display

**File**: `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

Rewrite the card body to show themed field groups. Use existing `DetailRow` component and format helpers. Only render a section if data exists.

### Themed sections (order as specified in issue):

1. **Core**: Name, type (with INSTITUTION_TYPES mapping), Carnegie Basic (code→label mapping in frontend)
2. **Location**: City/state (from top-level), locale, region, setting (carnegie_size)
3. **Academics**: Student-faculty ratio, full-time faculty rate
4. **Cost**: In-state tuition, out-of-state tuition, total price/myPrice, net price, room & board
5. **Admissions**: Acceptance rate, SAT range, ACT range, app fee, app platforms
6. **Outcomes**: Graduation rate, retention rate, median earnings, transfer rate
7. **Ranking**: All non-null rankings + blend score
8. **Athletics**: NCAA division, conference
9. **Demographics**: Total enrollment, undergrad enrollment, gender, minority-serving badges (HBCU, HSI, AANAPISI, PBI — show as Mantine `Badge` components only when true)
10. **Cultural Environment**: Partisan score/direction, religious affiliation, coeducational (from gender)
11. **Weather**: Climate group, temperature, precipitation
12. **Student Life**: Religious affiliation, coeducational, room & board
13. **Metadata**: School URL (as anchor link), accreditor, highest degree

### Code-to-label mappings (frontend constants):

- `INSTITUTION_TYPES`: Already exists — `{1: "Public", 2: "Private nonprofit", 3: "Private for-profit"}`
- `HIGHEST_DEGREES`: `{1: "Certificate", 2: "Associate's", 3: "Bachelor's", 4: "Master's", 5: "Doctorate"}`
- `LOCALE_LABELS`: `{11: "City: Large", 12: "City: Midsize", ...}` (from registry)
- `REGION_LABELS`: `{0: "U.S. Service Schools", 1: "New England", ...}`
- `NCAA_DIVISIONS`: `{1: "Division I", 2: "Division II", 3: "Division III"}`
- `KOPPEN_CLIMATE`: `{A: "Tropical", B: "Dry", C: "Temperate", D: "Continental", E: "Polar"}`

### Layout:
- `Modal` with `size="xl"` (wider than current "lg")
- Header: Name (brand.9) + City, State subtitle
- `SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 2 }}` for section pairs
- Each section: `Stack` with uppercase dimmed header + `DetailRow` entries
- Minority-serving badges: `Group` of `Badge variant="light"` components
- School URL: `<Anchor>` component

---

## Step 4: Update Tests

**File**: `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx`

- Remove GraphQL query mocking (no more `mockUseQuery` for InstitutionDetail itself)
- Still mock `urql` for `SimilarInstitutions` child component
- Change mock data shape from flat `InstitutionDetailData` to nested `SearchInstitution`
- Remove tests: loading state, error state, paused query
- Update tests: renders data, null institution, null optional fields
- Add tests: renders themed sections, minority-serving badges, school URL as link, code-to-label mappings, sections hidden when data is null

---

## Step 5: Backend Cleanup

- Remove `BuildInstitutionQuery()` from `api/internal/search/sql.go`
- Remove `Institution` resolver from `api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go`
- Remove `InstitutionDetail` type and `institution` query from `api/graph/schema/search.graphql`
- Remove `TestBuildInstitutionQuery` from `api/internal/search/search_test.go`
- Run `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`

---

## Step 6: Verify

**Note**: Step 2 (adding missing fields) depends on checking which JSONB keys actually exist in fixture data. If keys are missing from the data, skip those fields — they can be added later when data is sourced.

### Scoping decision

For fields that don't exist in the current data model (SAT, ACT, app fee, median earnings, minority-serving, school URL, accreditor, religious affiliation, Carnegie size/setting), I'll check the fixture data JSONB to see which keys are actually present before adding them to the schema. This avoids adding dead fields.

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx` | Rewrite: remove query, accept data prop, themed display |
| `web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.test.tsx` | Rewrite tests for new interface |
| `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` | Pass full institution to detail modal |
| `api/graph/schema/search.graphql` | Add new fields to types, remove InstitutionDetail |
| `api/graph/resolver/search_data_extract.go` | Add new extract functions |
| `api/graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go` | Wire new extractors, remove Institution resolver |
| `api/internal/search/sql.go` | Remove BuildInstitutionQuery |
| `api/internal/search/search_test.go` | Remove TestBuildInstitutionQuery |

## Verification

```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test
cd api && make test-unit
```

## Reusable Code

- `DetailRow` component — keep and reuse (InstitutionDetail.tsx:77-88)
- `formatPercent`, `formatCurrency`, `formatNumber` — keep and reuse
- `jsonbObject`, `jsonbString`, `jsonbInt`, `jsonbFloat`, `jsonbBool01`, `jsonbStringSlice` — reuse for new extract functions (search_data_extract.go)
- `INSTITUTION_TYPES` mapping — keep and reuse
- `renderWithMantine` from `web/src/test/render.tsx` — use in tests
